Internal Memorandum: MNPI controls for proposed acquisition and financing — Aurora Palm Berhad
The Company is negotiating to acquire 70% of Lapis Oils Sdn Bhd (Project Kenyalang) with an indicative purchase price range of MYR180–210 million and earn-out triggers linked to FY27 EBITDA; these terms are non-public and price-sensitive pending finalisation of the SPA.
An Islamic term financing term sheet with Cemerlang Nusantara Bank Berhad contemplates MYR220 million under Commodity Murabahah at SBR + 1.35% p.a. with a first charge over the Segamat processing plant; until definitive documentation is executed, these financing terms remain non-public.
Under Bursa Malaysia Main Market LR Paragraphs 9.03 and 9.04, an immediate announcement via BursaLINK is required once the SPA price and key terms are final, and no selective disclosure may occur prior to that time.
We are currently within a Closed Period commencing 16 May 2026 ahead of the targeted 15 June 2026 quarterly results announcement; directors and principal officers are restricted under Paragraph 14 and must not deal in the Company’s shares absent permitted exceptions and formal clearance.
For avoidance of doubt, our Immediate Announcement on BursaLINK dated 8 April 2026 disclosed a non-binding Heads of Agreement with Lapis Oils Sdn Bhd; that public filing is stale and does not cover the revised pricing and earn-out discussed internally on 26–27 May 2026.
Please note that Business Tropika’s 9 May 2026 article speculating about a private placement is media commentary; we have not mandated any placement and should not confirm or deny outside an approved Bursa disclosure.
Supplier update: Anjung Resin Ltd’s plant restart was communicated by its own press release on 2 March 2026; this is third-party public information and is not our MNPI.
Subtle risk: the internal slide deck labeled “Project Kenyalang v3.2” includes undisclosed capacity uplift assumptions that imply a material earnings impact; do not circulate outside wall-crossed counterparties and ensure NDA coverage.
Pursuant to CMSA s.188 and SC guidance, no insider may trade or tip others while in possession of this information; an insider list will be maintained and updated in real time.
Encik Hafiz bin Rahman (CFO, employee ID APB-4472) is the control owner for deal communications; direct queries to hafiz.rahman@aurorapalm.com.my and copy the Company Secretary, Puan Nor Aisyah binti Karim, at nor.aisyah@aurorapalm.com.my.
The financing security package includes an assignment of receivables from the Batu Lintang estate and a negative pledge; these items have not been announced and must be treated as MNPI.
Issuer-relative materiality assessment: the contemplated acquisition value equals approximately 48% of audited FY25 net assets and would likely require immediate announcement and, if thresholds are met, shareholder approval in general meeting per LR Chapter 10.
Action items: maintain blackout for all insiders until the Immediate Announcement; circulate wall-cross letters to potential lenders and advisers; prepare BursaLINK draft and Q&A for release within one market day of SPA signing.
This memo contains personal data processed for compliance purposes under the PDPA; do not forward beyond need-to-know distribution.
